code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test13-APR-2016Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mild hypoglycemia and low HbA1c, suggesting possible chronic low glucose or malnutrition. Elevated creatinine may indicate impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, which may suggest renal impairment and possible dehydration or metabolic disturbance. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend further evaluation of renal function, glycemic status, and nutritional assessment.
